reasoning_model_1: The audio generated by Model 1 fails completely in terms of Content Accuracy. The script required the model to say 'Really? Quick, tell me the details!', but the output only contains the repeated word 'details, details'. This is a severe omission and hallucination error, rendering the audio unusable. Consequently, the core paralinguistic feature (High Pitch) cannot be evaluated on the target text. According to the standard, empty or extremely poor content results in a score of 0.
reasoning_model_2: Model 2 generated the audio 'Really quick, tell me the details'. Regarding Content Accuracy, there is a noticeable deviation: the script 'Really? Quick...' (Interjection followed by Imperative) was read as 'Really quick...' (Adverbial phrase modifying the verb). This alters the grammatical structure and nuance of the sentence, though the core message remains understandable. Regarding the core feature (High Pitch), assuming the model attempted the feature, the content is at least present and intelligible. However, the alteration of the text prevents a perfect score. model_comparison: Model 1 is a complete failure with a score of 0 due to severe content omission (only outputting 'details, details'). Model 2 successfully generated the majority of the sentence, although it merged 'Really? Quick' into 'Really quick', changing the phrasing slightly. Since Model 1 failed the most basic requirement of content generation, Model 2 is the clear winner despite its minor content accuracy flaw.
